message:
docs(skills): require GitHub shorthand for PR links

Update the PR workflow skills to require owner/repo#number for PR cross-links instead of full GitHub URLs.

Also refresh the footer examples to use shorthand and keep full URLs only for deep links such as specific comments.

files.patch:
@@ -78,6 +78,10 @@ For Linear, use its magic words explicitly in that footer: - When there is a companion docs PR, add a plain line such as `📚 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261` to the same footer block rather than creating a dedicated section. +- When linking GitHub PRs in that footer, use GitHub shorthand such as + `tenzir/tenzir#5999` or `tenzir/docs#261` instead of full URLs. Use a full + URL only for deep links that shorthand cannot express, such as a specific + issue comment. Example footer: @@ -86,7 +90,7 @@ Example footer: 📚 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261
✅ Closes TNZ-150, TNZ-151
🎫 References TNZ-152
-📎 Related: https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir/issues/6048#issuecomment-1234567890 +📎 Related: tenzir/tenzir#5999 ```

@@ -33,6 +33,10 @@ Handle the operational workflow around docs.tenzir.com changes. `🛠️ Code PR: tenzir/tenzir#6048
🎫 References TNZ-150, TNZ-151`. - In the code PR, append or extend the final `...` footer with `📚 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261`. + - Use GitHub shorthand for PR links in both directions, such as + `tenzir/tenzir#6048` or `tenzir/docs#261`, instead of full URLs. Use full + URLs only for deep links that shorthand cannot express, such as a + specific comment. - Prefer that footer over dedicated `Code PR` or `Docs PR` sections. If a footer already exists, add another `
`-separated line instead of creating a second footer. @@ -68,7 +72,7 @@ Code PR example: 📚 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261
✅ Closes TNZ-150, TNZ-151
-📎 Related: https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir/issues/6048#issuecomment-1234567890 +📎 Related: tenzir/tenzir#5999 ```
